jokes.text: No, I'm just starting to realize I have about 6 months to live.
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing to multiple charity benefits for diseases his relatives had
jokes.punchline: Darkly calculating that he's apparently genetically doomed
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Larry Sanders logic - taking charity work to its morbid conclusion. Excellent economy and character-specific paranoia.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, I'm just starting to realize I have about 6 months to live.

jokes.text: I dragged joel grey about 20 yards before I realized he'd fallen down... talented man. Father of actress Jennifer grey.
jokes.setup: Recounting participating in celebrity three-legged race
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions dragging an unconscious Joel Grey, then adds irrelevant trivia
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy and Larry's oblivious self-centeredness. The Jennifer Grey detail is perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I dragged joel grey about 20 yards before I realized he'd fallen down... talented man. Father of actress Jennifer grey.

jokes.text: Charity begins at home, except in Hollywood where it begins at 7 pm every night in every fucking hotel ballroom in town.
jokes.setup: Larry declaring he'll only do benefits for causes he believes in
jokes.punchline: Artie's cynical take on Hollywood charity circuit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - cynical, witty, and cutting through Hollywood BS. Great twist on familiar saying.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Charity begins at home, except in Hollywood where it begins at 7 pm every night in every fucking hotel ballroom in town.

jokes.text: This is about my personal beliefs, not money.
jokes.setup: Larry just heard about a lucrative New York benefit
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself after hearing the money
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry hypocrisy - stating principles then immediately abandoning them for money.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is about my personal beliefs, not money.

jokes.text: What, are they kidding? No, that's what it says. Cheapskates. All right, call them. Tell them maybe if they send a helicopter. Otherwise, no dice.
jokes.setup: Disabled voters coalition offering $2,500
jokes.punchline: Larry's outrageous helicopter demand for disabled charity
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's entitlement and obliviousness to irony of demanding helicopter for disabled charity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What, are they kidding? No, that's what it says. Cheapskates. All right, call them. Tell them maybe if they send a helicopter. Otherwise, no dice.

jokes.text: 5 years. Come on, guys. Where's the cure?
jokes.setup: Hank justifying dropping Alzheimer's charity
jokes.punchline: Blaming Alzheimer's researchers for not curing it fast enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - treating medical research like a TV show that should be cancelled for low ratings.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: 5 years. Come on, guys. Where's the cure?

jokes.text: Yes! Firm date, get it! Puerto Rico!
jokes.setup: Beverly mentions getting firm date for blood disease weekend
jokes.punchline: Larry excited about 'firm' pun while thinking about vacation perks
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but shows Larry's juvenile enthusiasm for wordplay and vacation perks.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yes! Firm date, get it! Puerto Rico!

jokes.text: Oh, right, uh... nothing contagious.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Beverly to remember something
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry's shallow criterion for choosing charities
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's self-preservation over altruism.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, right, uh... nothing contagious.

jokes.text: I should take a look at his thumb. Did they do a nice job? Fan-fucking-tastic.
jokes.setup: Artie mentioning gaffer's reattached thumb
jokes.punchline: Larry treating surgical reattachment like home renovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness - treating serious injury like entertainment while missing the real priority (cake).

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I should take a look at his thumb. Did they do a nice job?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fan-fucking-tastic.

jokes.text: No, I mean about the cake.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for details about thumb reattachment
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only cares about the cake details
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Larry's priorities perfectly - medical miracle vs dessert quality.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, I mean about the cake.

jokes.text: Good! And your hair's the same color as last week. That's good. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Paula delivering Rob Reiner news
jokes.punchline: Random hair color comment creating uncomfortable beat
jokes.explanation: Awkward workplace small talk that goes nowhere - typical Larry Sanders uncomfortable interaction.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Good! And your hair's the same color as last week. That's good. Thank you.

jokes.text: You know, that happens to creative geniuses, to all except my good friend professor Irwin corey.
jokes.setup: Discussing Rob Reiner becoming serious as director
jokes.punchline: Exception of ancient vaudeville comedian who never became serious
jokes.explanation: Artie's old-school showbiz reference - mildly amusing but requires knowledge of obscure performer.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know, that happens to creative geniuses, to all except my good friend professor Irwin corey.

jokes.text: You'll be able to tell there's no head on the horse.
jokes.setup: Discussing Western sketch problems
jokes.punchline: Larry worried about obvious production limitations being visible
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about show's low production values, characteristic Larry neurosis about appearances.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You'll be able to tell there's no head on the horse.

jokes.text: driving your new cat down the Santa Monica freeway.
jokes.setup: Reading cue card about Mike Ditka's new car
jokes.punchline: Misreading 'car' as 'cat' creating absurd image
jokes.explanation: Simple misreading joke, shows Larry's preparation issues but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: driving your new cat down the Santa Monica freeway.

jokes.text: My balls are killing me.
jokes.setup: Larry in Western costume preparation
jokes.punchline: Crude complaint about costume discomfort
jokes.explanation: Generic physical discomfort joke, relies on crude humor without much craft or character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My balls are killing me.

jokes.text: There goes Mr. Reiner. I can see roberino disappearing into the clouds right now.
jokes.setup: Larry passing on nerve deafness benefit
jokes.punchline: Artie's dramatic visualization of lost guest opportunity
jokes.explanation: Artie's theatrical Hollywood dramatics about losing celebrity guest, mildly amusing escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There goes Mr. Reiner. I can see roberino disappearing into the clouds right now.

jokes.text: One hand washes the other. Scratch my back, I'll scratch yours. You know, tit for tat. Done with your metaphors? Mas o menos, hombre.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining Hollywood quid pro quo
jokes.punchline: Piling on clichés then switching to Spanish when asked to stop
jokes.explanation: Artie's verbose nature on display, piling metaphors then deflecting with Spanish - characteristic behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: One hand washes the other. Scratch my back, I'll scratch yours. You know, tit for tat.
Done with your metaphors?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Mas o menos, hombre.

jokes.text: Who are you, big Chief lenscrafters?
jokes.setup: Hank wearing glasses with Native American costume
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate but clever glasses/Native American mashup
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining LensCrafters with stereotypical Native American title, though culturally insensitive.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Who are you, big Chief lenscrafters?

jokes.text: Too much. It looks like one of those stage productions of cats.
jokes.setup: Hank's excessive Native American face paint
jokes.punchline: Comparing to theatrical Cats makeup
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy about overdone makeup, decent comparison to theatrical excess.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Too much. It looks like one of those stage productions of cats.

jokes.text: That says 'applesauce.' No, it says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Hank misreading cue card
jokes.punchline: Confusing applause with applesauce
jokes.explanation: Simple misreading joke showing Hank's incompetence, but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That says 'applesauce.'

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, it says 'applause.'

jokes.text: When he sits around the house... A winnebago without drapes.
jokes.setup: Classic fat joke setup about guest
jokes.punchline: Interrupted mid-joke, then later non-sequitur about dress/drapes
jokes.explanation: Hacky fat joke interrupted, then weird dress comparison - shows Hank's poor comedy instincts.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: When he sits around the house... A winnebago without drapes.

jokes.text: And tell them I have some nerve deafness. Hank, that's so sad. I really don't have nerve deafness. I know. That's what's so sad.
jokes.setup: Hank claiming to have nerve deafness for authenticity
jokes.punchline: Beverly's sympathy is for his lying, not fake condition
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Beverly's sympathy is for his pathetic dishonesty, not the fake disability.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And tell them I have some nerve deafness.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Hank, that's so sad.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I really don't have nerve deafness.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I know. That's what's so sad.

jokes.text: Boy, finally some good news for Michael Jackson.
jokes.setup: Study about children left home alone
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about Michael Jackson's alleged behavior
jokes.explanation: Topical but cheap shot at easy target, lacks cleverness and feels dated even for the time.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Boy, finally some good news for Michael Jackson.

jokes.text: Is it last january already? No. It sounds like it from that joke.
jokes.setup: Writer defending old Michael Jackson joke
jokes.punchline: Artie pointing out joke's staleness
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy writing process, Artie's sharp eye for stale material.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: Is it last january already? No.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It sounds like it from that joke.

jokes.text: How cinematic! I can almost see him falling.
jokes.setup: Hearing about Reiner's stair-falling excuse
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically praising the visual drama of fake excuse
jokes.explanation: Larry's sarcasm about obviously fabricated Hollywood excuse, good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: How cinematic! I can almost see him falling.

jokes.text: And people wonder why I'm a cynical asshole. No, they don't.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his cynicism
jokes.punchline: Artie's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie deadpan - nobody wonders about Larry's cynicism because it's obvious. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And people wonder why I'm a cynical asshole.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No, they don't.

jokes.text: You guys rousted me out of bed because Pauly Shore wasn't available?
jokes.setup: Belzer arriving as last-minute replacement
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he's even lower on call list than Pauly Shore
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy from Belzer about his status in Hollywood hierarchy.

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: You guys rousted me out of bed because Pauly Shore wasn't available?

jokes.text: I don't like kiwi. Oh, sayib, I should have known! We've got a mango flying in, chop-chop.
jokes.setup: Belzer rejecting kiwi fruit
jokes.punchline: Beverly's ridiculous promise of airborne mango delivery
jokes.explanation: Beverly's absurd escalation trying to accommodate guest preferences, good character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: I don't like kiwi.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Oh, sayib, I should have known! We've got a mango flying in, chop-chop.

jokes.text: Did you know that spinal tap was mostly improvised? Did you know that? Really? And what do you think I'm doing now? I'm making this up.
jokes.setup: Larry asking prepared question about improvisation
jokes.punchline: Belzer pointing out the irony while actually improvising
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on improvisation while demonstrating it, Belzer breaking the fourth wall effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Did you know that spinal tap was mostly improvised? Did you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And what do you think I'm doing now?

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: I'm making this up.

jokes.text: You'll have security stop me on the way out? Just don't take the ones with the pictures. Your picture? Yeah. Don't worry about it. I'll get you another.
jokes.setup: Belzer wanting to take show mug
jokes.punchline: Beverly's pathetic attempts to control minor theft
jokes.explanation: Petty workplace politics over promotional mugs, shows Beverly's ineffectual authority.

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: You'll have security stop me on the way out?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Just don't take the ones with the pictures.

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: Your picture?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Belzer
dialogue.line: Don't worry about it. I'll get you another.

jokes.text: I think that every time I watch that America's funniest people. That just about breaks your spirit.
jokes.setup: Larry asking what's wrong with human race
jokes.punchline: Blaming America's Funniest Home Videos for spiritual damage
jokes.explanation: Larry's highbrow disdain for lowbrow TV, characteristic cultural snobbery.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think that every time I watch that America's funniest people. That just about breaks your spirit.

jokes.text: I'll rub his face in it, Artie. You know that? I'll look like the bigger man, make him feel like shit.
jokes.setup: Larry decides to do the benefit Reiner suggested
jokes.punchline: Plans to be vindictively charitable to seem magnanimous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry psychology - turning charity into revenge while maintaining plausible deniability. Brilliant character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'll rub his face in it, Artie. You know that? I'll look like the bigger man, make him feel like shit.

jokes.text: A few good men... want a good courtroom movie? Caine mutiny. Yeah, bogey with the steel balls.
jokes.setup: Dismissing Reiner's famous film
jokes.punchline: Prefers older Bogart film, mentions steel balls callback
jokes.explanation: Decent old-school movie reference with subtle callback, though not particularly distinctive.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A few good men... want a good courtroom movie? Caine mutiny. Yeah, bogey with the steel balls.

jokes.text: The world makes me sad. What the... what? Sad.
jokes.setup: Hank's dramatic speech about charity focus
jokes.punchline: Confused reaction to his own dramatic statement
jokes.explanation: Hank's fake profundity followed by confusion shows his shallow performance of depth.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: The world makes me sad. What the... what? Sad.

jokes.text: Let's say, for the sake of my argument, that they get that.
jokes.setup: Hank unsure if kids get nerve deafness
jokes.punchline: Deciding to assume they do to continue his speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - prioritizing his dramatic speech over factual accuracy about the charity.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Let's say, for the sake of my argument, that they get that.

jokes.text: How many people... famous people... went number 2 tonight? You don't have to tell me. I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Hank's emotional speech about charity
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Random bathroom humor disrupting serious moment - shows Hank's inability to maintain tone.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: How many people... famous people... went number 2 tonight? You don't have to tell me. I'm just kidding.

jokes.text: Larry, do you honestly think I would cancel at the last second without a legitimate excuse?
jokes.setup: Larry confronting Reiner about cancellation
jokes.punchline: Reiner's defensive question after being caught
jokes.explanation: Reiner's wounded dignity when caught in obvious lie, mild irony of his indignation.

dialogue.speaker: Rob Reiner
dialogue.line: Larry, do you honestly think I would cancel at the last second without a legitimate excuse?

jokes.text: I am uncomfortable discussing size in the bathroom, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Reiner calling Larry 'the bigger man'
jokes.punchline: Larry's uncomfortable sexual interpretation in bathroom setting
jokes.explanation: Larry's sexual anxiety creating awkwardness from innocent phrase, characteristic neurosis.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I am uncomfortable discussing size in the bathroom, you know what I mean?

jokes.text: Toilet paper is always very funny to everyone except the French. I don't think those bidets are sanitary at all, do you?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Reiner's bathroom encounter
jokes.punchline: Random toilet paper cultural observation and bidet criticism
jokes.explanation: Random bathroom humor observations that don't land well, feels forced and disconnected.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Toilet paper is always very funny to everyone except the French. I don't think those bidets are sanitary at all, do you?

jokes.text: I don't think that I told him I was there to rub his face in it and make him feel like shit, so I don't feel completely, totally truthful.
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on his honest conversation with Reiner
jokes.punchline: Realizes he wasn't completely honest because he didn't mention his vindictive motives
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Larry's warped sense of honesty - feels guilty for not admitting his petty motivations.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't think that I told him I was there to rub his face in it and make him feel like shit, so I don't feel completely, totally truthful.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. That's what ruined my second marriage. I told the truth. I found it to be disastrous.
jokes.setup: Larry considering being honest with Reiner
jokes.punchline: Artie's cynical marriage advice against honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie wisdom - cynical but probably accurate advice about relationships and honesty.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Let me tell you something. That's what ruined my second marriage. I told the truth. I found it to be disastrous.

jokes.text: You can't handle the truth. This. It isn't funny. This is hilarious. Ha ha. See?
jokes.setup: Artie quoting 'A Few Good Men' line
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating prop comedy's failure then forced laughter
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about comedy failing then forcing laughter, references earlier Reiner discussion.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You can't handle the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: This. It isn't funny.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: This is hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Ha ha. See?

jokes.text: Maybe you can get that woman from the zoo and she can bring an animal to shit on me.
jokes.setup: Reiner wanting to avoid serious interview topics
jokes.punchline: Extreme preference for slapstick humiliation over career discussion
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Reiner's desire to avoid serious discussion, memorably specific image.

dialogue.speaker: Rob Reiner
dialogue.line: Maybe you can get that woman from the zoo and she can bring an animal to shit on me.

jokes.text: Rob? Mr. Reiner won't be joining us this evening. He just canceled.
jokes.setup: Larry's honest confession to Reiner
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequences of truth-telling
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Larry's honesty experiment - immediate negative consequences prove Artie right.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Rob? Mr. Reiner won't be joining us this evening. He just canceled.

jokes.text: Of course, I anticipated your inability to keep your mouth shut. So I arranged a backup.
jokes.setup: Larry's shock at Reiner canceling
jokes.punchline: Artie's prescient preparation for Larry's predictable behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - knowing Larry so well he prepared for his inevitable self-sabotage. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Of course, I anticipated your inability to keep your mouth shut. So I arranged a backup.

jokes.text: I actually do more improvising in the movie than I do here.
jokes.setup: Pauly discussing his improvisation
jokes.punchline: Admitting his scripted TV appearance is less spontaneous than his movies
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-commentary on performance authenticity, but not particularly sharp or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Pauly Shore
dialogue.line: I actually do more improvising in the movie than I do here.

jokes.text: When will you book me again? When another big, huge guest cancels? Maybe within the month.
jokes.setup: Pauly asking about future bookings
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest assessment of Pauly's backup status
jokes.explanation: Larry's casual cruelty about Pauly's low status, characteristic insensitive honesty.

dialogue.speaker: Pauly Shore
dialogue.line: When will you book me again?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: When another big, huge guest cancels? Maybe within the month.

jokes.text: They offered you 10 grand? Yeah. Beverly.
jokes.setup: Larry discovering nerve deafness benefit paid $10,000
jokes.punchline: Realizing he gave up huge money for principles
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Larry's horror at discovering the financial cost of his moral stance. Great callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They offered you 10 grand?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Beverly.
